mnemonics: Concurrency Has No Single Point (CHaNSP) - Remember the four key characteristics: Concurrency, Heterogeneity, No global clock, Scalability, and Point (Single Point of) failure.
keyPoints: A distributed system is a collection of independent computers that appears to its users as a single coherent system.
Key goals are to provide transparency, openness, and scalability.
Systems are characterized by concurrency of components, lack of a global clock, and independent failure of components.
commonMistakes: Assuming a distributed system is just a networked system; it must appear as a single system to the user.
Believing that components in a distributed system are homogeneous; heterogeneity is a common characteristic.
Forgetting that partial failure (where some components fail while others continue) is a fundamental challenge.
